diagnosis.description: Patient presents with elevated body temperature (101Â°F), fatigue, and mild throat irritation. No bacterial infection detected. Likely viral etiology.

lab_report.remarks: All values within normal range, supports viral infection diagnosis

diagnosis.description: Elevated fasting glucose and HbA1c levels indicate poor glycemic control over last 3 months.

lab_report.remarks: Above normal glucose levels, requires monitoring

diagnosis.description: Consistently high blood pressure readings over multiple visits. Risk of cardiovascular complications.
Low hemoglobin and ferritin levels suggest iron deficiency, likely due to poor dietary intake.
Persistent cough and chest congestion. Chest X-ray shows mild inflammation.
Low thyroid hormone levels causing fatigue, weight gain, and slow metabolism.
Ultrasound reveals 5mm stone in left kidney causing intermittent pain.
Recurring headaches with sensitivity to light and sound. Neurological exam normal.
Ultrasound indicates fatty deposits in liver. Likely due to diet and lifestyle.
Symptoms include burning urination and frequent urge. Lab confirms bacterial infection.
Inflammation of stomach lining due to irregular eating habits.
Low bone density increasing fracture risk.
Patient experiences shortness of breath and wheezing, especially at night. Triggered by dust exposure.
Irregular menstrual cycles and hormonal imbalance indicating Polycystic Ovary Syndrome.
Chest pain and reduced blood flow to heart detected through angiography.
Sneezing, runny nose, and nasal congestion due to pollen allergy.
Gradual loss of kidney function over time, elevated creatinine levels.
Low vitamin D levels causing fatigue and bone pain.
Interrupted breathing during sleep causing daytime fatigue.
Persistent low mood, lack of interest, and fatigue over several weeks.
Joint pain and swelling due to uric acid crystal deposition.
High fever, joint pain, and low platelet count indicating dengue infection.
Patient has fever, cough with sputum, and chest pain. Chest X-ray confirms lung infection.
Red rashes and itching due to allergic reaction to cosmetics.
Elevated cholesterol levels increasing risk of heart disease.
Joint pain and stiffness, especially in the morning.
Prolonged fever and abdominal discomfort. Blood test confirms infection.
Frequent anxiety, restlessness, and sleep disturbances.
Chronic breathing difficulty due to long-term smoking.
Severe acne due to hormonal imbalance.
Chronic lower back pain due to prolonged sitting.
Ultrasound shows stones in gallbladder causing abdominal pain.

patients.name: Arvind Menon

diagnosis.description: Patient reports exertional chest pain radiating to left arm, associated with shortness of breath. ECG shows ST segment depression and T-wave inversion. Risk factors include long-standing hypertension and smoking. Likely reduced coronary blood flow due to arterial narrowing.

prescription: Nitroglycerin as needed for chest pain
Lifestyle modification including smoking cessation and diet control

lab_report.remarks: Findings suggest myocardial ischemia; cardiology follow-up required

diagnosis.description: Patient experiences frequent unilateral throbbing headaches lasting 6-12 hours, accompanied by nausea and photophobia. Episodes triggered by stress and lack of sleep. Neurological examination is normal, ruling out secondary causes.

lab_report.remarks: Primary migraine disorder; no structural abnormalities detected

diagnosis.description: Long-term smoker presenting with chronic cough, sputum production, and progressive breathlessness. Spirometry shows reduced FEV1/FVC ratio. Symptoms worsen during winter months.

lab_report.remarks: Moderate to severe COPD; requires long-term management

diagnosis.description: Patient has poorly controlled diabetes with complaints of tingling and numbness in feet. HbA1c significantly elevated indicating chronic hyperglycemia leading to nerve damage.

lab_report.remarks: Diabetic neuropathy confirmed; strict glycemic control required

diagnosis.description: Symptoms include fatigue, cold intolerance, and weight gain. Lab findings show elevated TSH and low T4. Presence of anti-thyroid antibodies suggests autoimmune thyroiditis (Hashimoto's disease).
Patient complains of burning epigastric pain, especially on an empty stomach. Endoscopy reveals ulcer in the gastric lining. Likely associated with H. pylori infection and NSAID use.
Patient noticed a painless lump in the breast. Ultrasound suggests a benign tumor. No signs of malignancy observed.
Patient with history of alcohol use presents with jaundice, ascites, and fatigue. Liver function tests show significant abnormalities indicating chronic liver damage.
Patient presents with extreme fatigue, dizziness, and pallor. Hemoglobin significantly reduced. Likely due to chronic blood loss or inadequate dietary intake.
Persistent cough for over 3 weeks, weight loss, night sweats, and fever. Sputum test positive for Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

Patient reports chronic joint pain, swelling, and morning stiffness lasting more than 1 hour. Symptoms are symmetrical, affecting both hands and wrists. Autoimmune etiology suspected due to elevated inflammatory markers.
Patient presents with shortness of breath, swelling in legs, and fatigue. Echocardiography shows reduced ejection fraction indicating impaired cardiac function.
Frequent episodes of burning urination and urgency. Previous history of UTIs suggests recurrent infection likely due to incomplete treatment or hygiene factors.
Patient presents with mild abdominal discomfort and elevated liver enzymes. Ultrasound indicates fatty infiltration of liver not related to alcohol consumption.
Irregular menstrual cycles, weight gain, and acne. Hormonal imbalance confirmed with elevated androgen levels and ovarian cysts seen on ultrasound.
Patient exhibits tremors, rigidity, and slowed movements. Neurological examination indicates progressive neurodegenerative disorder.
Severe abdominal pain radiating to the back, associated with nausea and vomiting. Likely triggered by gallstones or alcohol consumption.
Small lump detected in breast during routine screening. Biopsy confirms malignant cells but limited to localized area without metastasis.
Patient complains of facial pain, nasal congestion, and headache. Symptoms worsen when bending forward. Likely bacterial sinus infection.
Degenerative joint disease causing knee pain, stiffness, and reduced mobility. Symptoms worsen with activity and improve with rest.
